generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor actuator of the MABI MAX 100 first axis"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder attached to the motor shaft of the MABI MAX 100 first axis to measure angular position with high resolution"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EncoderPositionData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder in the drive train of the MABI MAX 100 first axis for redundancy and reduced positional uncertainty"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EncoderPositionData
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance EncoderPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance TorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
    base:desc "Derived torque measurement calculated from motor current using known motor constants"
]

// C10: Models/Data - Engineering Models
instance FirstAxisStructuralBDD : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram modeling the structural components of the MABI MAX 100 first axis including motor, gearbox (cycloidal gear), and drive train connections"
]

instance FirstAxisStructuralIBD : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram detailing rotational torque/velocity and electrical current flow definitions for the first axis components"
]

// C10: Models/Data - Simulation Models
instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating friction behavior using Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics for the first axis drive train"
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing Stribeck friction characteristics to simulate dynamic friction behavior in the first axis"
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model representing Dahl friction model for complex friction analysis in the first axis"
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model using LuGre friction model to simulate both static and dynamic friction components in the drive train"
]

instance IdealSystemBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating torque/velocity behavior without friction components for comparison purposes"
]

// C10: Data Traces
instance LiveMotorCurrentTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time motor current data stream from encoder measurements for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

instance LiveMotorAngleTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time angular position data from encoder measurements for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EncoderPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance LiveTorqueTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time torque measurement derived from motor current using known motor constants for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

instance LiveAngularVelocityTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time angular velocity data from encoder measurements for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EncoderPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance SimulatedTorqueTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated torque output trace generated by processing real-time data through friction models"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedAngularVelocityTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated angular velocity trace generated by friction models for comparison with live measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiveAngularVelocityTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance FrictionCharacteristicsMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Metadata trace tracking data origins (sensor channels, model sources) for traceability of friction analysis results"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiveMotorCurrentTrace, LiveTorqueTrace, SimulatedTorqueTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnalysisEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for streaming real-time sensor data (motor current, angle) from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller to digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamEnabler
]

instance SimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation classes processing input data traces through friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) to generate simulated torque/velocity traces"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance FrictionAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component analyzing live data traces against friction models to identify static, kinetic, and viscous friction characteristics"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard visualization platform displaying live vs. simulated data traces (torque, angular velocity) and friction analysis results"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance DataStreamEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data streaming component handling MQTT protocol for sensor data transmission"
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time friction analysis service continuously identifying and refining white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models using live operational data"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeFrictionInsights, ModelRefinementAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dashboard-based visualization service comparing measured vs. simulated data traces (torque, angular velocity) for friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLossVisualization, ModelAccuracyVisualization
]

instance ModelIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling seamless integration of structural models (SysML BDD/IBD) and simulation models with the digital shadow components"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceability
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeFrictionInsights : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of static, kinetic, and viscous friction characteristics in the MABI MAX 100 first axis drive train"
]

instance ModelRefinementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Continuous identification and refinement of friction models (white-box/black-box) through iterative comparison of measured vs. simulated data traces"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionLossVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of friction losses in torque and angular velocity traces for motion accuracy optimization"
]

instance ModelAccuracyVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Dashboard visualization comparing model accuracy between measured and simulated data traces to support control parameter adjustments"
]

instance ControlParameterAdjustment : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of control parameters based on friction analysis insights to improve robot motion accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DataTraceability : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Metadata tracking system ensuring traceability between data sources, models, and processing components for friction analysis"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is real-time, with data acquisition and processing occurring at the frequency of sensor streams (e.g., motor current and angle updates). The digital shadow dynamically exchanges friction models during operation, ensuring real-time synchronization between physical and virtual representations. The primary focus is on operational-time-scale analysis, with no explicit mention of offline or batch processing phases."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation consists of a **single digital shadow** focused on the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot. It integrates:
  - The physical system (first axis: motor, gearbox, cycloidal gear).
  - A set of engineering, simulation, and data calculation models (SysML BDD/IBD, Simulink models).
  - A processing component (Simulation_M, Simulation_GW, Simulation_GB) that dynamically exchanges friction models.
  - A dashboard for visualization and analysis.
  The constellation is centralized and purpose-driven, with no mention of external DTs or horizontal integration with other systems."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates during the **operated** stage of the system’s lifecycle, specifically targeting the **as-operated** representation of the robot’s first axis. It does not cover the **as-designed** or **as-manufactured** stages, as these would require structural and manufacturing-specific models. The primary purpose is to analyze friction behavior in real-time during operation."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity considerations include:
  - **Data Traceability**: All data traces (measured/simulated) are linked to their sources (e.g., sensor channels, model inputs) via metadata, ensuring transparency in friction model derivation.
  - **Model Validation**: Simulation models (Simulink) are validated against live operational data to identify discrepancies (e.g., friction losses) between measured and simulated torque/velocity traces.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: The use of two encoders per drive axis (motor and secondary) reduces positional uncertainty and backlash, improving the accuracy of friction measurements.
  - **Verification/Validation**: No explicit mention of formal verification or validation frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards), but the methodology relies on iterative comparison between measured and simulated data."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s technical connection to the physical system relies on:
  - **Data Acquisition**: MQTT protocol for streaming sensor data (motor current I_M, output angle) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller.
  - **Model Integration**: Asset Administration Shells (AAS) for linking digital shadow components (models, data traces) to the physical system’s structure (SysML BDD/IBD).
  - **Real-Time Processing**: Live data is processed in real-time by simulation models (Simulink) to generate friction analysis outputs, ensuring synchronization with the physical system."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin incorporates:
  - **SysML** for structural modeling (BDD/IBD).
  - **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for simulation models.
  - **MQTT** for data acquisition.
  - **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** for digital shadow integration.
  No explicit mention of broader standards (e.g., ISO 4180 for digital twins, IEC 62264 for industrial automation), but the methodology aligns with MBSE and digital shadow reference models (e.g., [15])."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s security and safety considerations include:
  - **Data Security**: MQTT protocol for streaming sensor data, though no explicit mention of encryption or access controls.
  - **Safety**: The robot’s safety cell restricts the working range to [-90°, 90°], and the maximum speed is 75°/s. The digital shadow does not enable remote operation or autonomous intervention in the physical system, mitigating safety risks.
  - **Operational Safety**: The focus is on improving motion accuracy via friction modeling, with no mention of autonomous decision-making or emergency interventions."
]
